# CSV Tools – Copilot Workspace Instructions

## Stack

- **Framework**: TanStack Start (React 19, SSR)
- **Routing**: TanStack Router – file-based, auto-generated. Routes live in `src/routes/`. **Never manually edit `src/routeTree.gen.ts`** – it is regenerated by the Vite plugin on every dev server start.
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4.1 with CSS custom properties. Always use the shorthand variable syntax – `text-(--sea-ink)` not `text-[var(--sea-ink)]`. Design tokens are defined in `src/styles.css`.
- **Validation**: Valibot (modular, tree-shakeable). Schemas are built dynamically in `src/utils/schema-builder.ts`.
- **CSV parsing**: PapaParse via `src/utils/csv.ts`. CSV row values are always `string | undefined`.
- **i18n**: ParaglideJS. Message keys live in `messages/en.json` and `messages/es.json`. Any user-visible string added to the UI must have a key in both files.
- **Deployment**: Cloudflare Workers via Wrangler.

## Project Structure

```
src/
  routes/          # One file per route (TanStack Router file-based)
  features/        # Feature-scoped code
    validator/
      components/  # FileUpload, SchemaBuilder, ValidationResults
      hooks/       # Custom hooks (e.g. useFileUpload). Business logic extracted from components.
  components/      # Shared layout components (Header, Footer, etc.)
  utils/           # Pure utility functions (csv.ts, schema-builder.ts)
  types/           # Shared TypeScript types (validator.ts)
  styles.css       # Design tokens and global styles
tests/             # All test files (Vitest). Mirror src/ structure by name.
samples/           # Sample CSV files for manual and integration testing
```

## Conventions

- **Imports**: Always use the `@/` path alias (e.g. `@/types/validator`). Never use relative paths (`../`, `../../`).
- **TypeScript generics**: Must match `/^(T|T[A-Z][A-Za-z]+)$/` – use `TKey`, `TValue`, not `K`, `V`.
- **New features**: Go under `src/features/<feature-name>/`. Shared UI only in `src/components/`.
- **Hooks**: Business logic that would otherwise live in a component goes in a custom hook under `src/features/<feature-name>/hooks/`. Components should be focused on JSX/UI; hooks own state, effects, and side effects. Hook files are named `use<Name>.ts`.
- **New routes**: Create `src/routes/<name>.tsx` using `createFileRoute`. The route tree regenerates automatically.
- **Tests**: All test files go in `tests/`. Run with `npm test`. Integration tests may read from `samples/`.
- **Linting/formatting**: `npm run lint` (ESLint) and `npm run format` (Prettier). Husky enforces these on commit.

## Commands

```bash
npm run dev       # Start dev server (also triggers route tree codegen)
npm test          # Run all tests (Vitest)
npm run typecheck # tsc --noEmit
npm run lint      # ESLint
npm run format    # Prettier
npm run build     # Production build
npm run deploy    # Build + wrangler deploy to Cloudflare Workers
npm run codegen   # Recompile ParaglideJS i18n messages
```

## Accessibility

**Semantic HTML first — reach for ARIA only when no HTML equivalent exists.**

- Use the right HTML element before adding `role`, `aria-*`, or JS interaction:
  - Interactive triggers → `<button type="button">` (handles Enter, Space, focus natively)
  - Form control groups → `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` (not `div[role="group"]`)
  - Label association → wrap `<label>text<input/></label>` (not `htmlFor`/`id` pairs)
  - Page regions → `<main>`, `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>` (not `div[role]`)
- ARIA is appropriate only when semantic HTML cannot express the meaning:
  - Live regions: `role="status"` / `role="alert"` / `aria-live` for dynamic announcements
  - `aria-current="step"` on active step in a multi-step indicator
  - `aria-hidden={true}` on decorative icons (e.g. Lucide icons that duplicate adjacent text)
- **File upload pattern**: use a hidden `<input type="file">` triggered by a `<button>` — no `div[role="button"]`. Use Pragmatic DnD (`@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop`) for the drop zone; it handles `onDragLeave` child-element flickering and `preventUnhandled` correctly.
- Do not add ARIA attributes to fix problems that should be solved with the correct HTML element.
